task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Original image is loaded in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A grayscale image (either a complex photo or a synthetic image with shapes and text) is loaded and visible on the GIMP canvas.
The 'Threshold' dialog box is open and visible over the GIMP interface, or the 'Threshold' option is being selected from the 'Colors' menu.
The image on the canvas shows a stark black and white preview with no smooth gray gradients, indicating the threshold effect is being applied or adjusted.

task_completion.description: Binary conversion is finalized on the canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Threshold dialog is no longer visible (OK was clicked), and the image on the GIMP canvas is finalized as pure black and white.

integrity.description: Agent used the GIMP GUI for conversion
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the GIMP graphical interface to perform the task, rather than using terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The image is a genuine threshold of the original
integrity.visual_evidence: The black and white image on the canvas is clearly a thresholded version of the original image (preserving its structural outlines), rather than a newly drawn or unrelated image.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually run the export script
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not manually execute the export script in the terminal, as exporting is handled automatically by the environment.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires converting a grayscale image to pure black and white using GIMP's Threshold tool. The original image is either a complex photo from Unsplash or a synthetic image with shapes and text. A successful conversion results in an image with >=85% pure black/white pixels and <15% mid-tones (stark binary appearance). The agent only needs to apply the threshold in GIMP; exporting is automated. Verify that the final image on the canvas is a genuine black and white threshold of the original image.
